send_money_existing_user.name: Send Money — Existing User (Saved Recipient + Card)
send_money_existing_user.description: Happy path for returning users who already have a saved recipient and payment method. This is the shortest and most common transaction path.

steps.note: User opens send money. On mobile includes anonymous users.
System generates rate/fee quotes for all payment × delivery combinations. Can fire multiple times as user changes amounts.
User picks delivery method (Bank, Cash, Mobile Wallet).
INTENT SIGNAL. User accepts quote and proceeds. On mobile, filters out anonymous browsers.
User picks existing saved recipient.
User picks saved payment method (card, bank, etc.).
User enters CVV for card payment. Conditional: card payments only.
User reviews final details and taps Send.
3DS/card auth initiated. Conditional: card payments only.
Card auth succeeds. Conditional: card payments only.
Server-side: transaction accepted. Revenue event.

send_money_existing_user.platform_context: Mobile top-of-funnel includes anonymous users. Fair comparison starts at Quote Confirmed. Post-intent: iOS 78% > Android 74% > Web 70%.
critical_dropoffs: Quote Created → Quote Payout Method Selected: massive drop on mobile because most are anonymous browsers
Payment Method Selected → CVV Entered: many users use non-card methods (open banking, Apple Pay)

send_money_recipient_first.name: Send Money — Recipient First (Existing User, Mobile)
send_money_recipient_first.description: User starts from their recipient list (not the quote page). This is the highest-converting funnel because these are returning users with saved recipients who know who they want to send to. Mobile only (iOS + Android).

steps.note: User opens recipient list. flowName distinguishes entry point: 'recipientFirstHome' (home page 'see all'), 'sendMoneyQuoteFirst' (via send money), or location='recipient-list' (Account → Recipients).
Quote generated for selected corridor.
User accepts the quote.
User picks their saved recipient. flowName attribute matches the flow entry.
User provides transfer reason (required for compliance in many corridors).
User picks payment method (saved card, Apple Pay, bank transfer, etc.).
Final review and confirmation.
Transaction accepted.

send_money_recipient_first.note: This is the BEST converting funnel (41.8%) because it's existing users who already know who they're sending to. The flowName attribute on Recipients Accessed and Recipient Selected was enhanced in the current release to distinguish entry points: 'recipientFirstHome' = home page recent recipients, 'sendMoneyQuoteFirst' = via send money flow, location='recipient-list' = Account → Recipients.
critical_dropoffs: Recipients Accessed → Quote Created: 77,699 → 58,108 (75%) — some users browse recipients without proceeding
Quote Created → Quote Confirmed: 58,108 → 38,957 (67%) — rate/fee review step, some users don't like what they see
Rest of funnel is tight: 97%+ step-to-step after Recipient Selected

send_money_new_user.name: Send Money — New User (Creates Recipient)
send_money_new_user.description: Path for users who need to create a new recipient. Longer funnel with bank details entry — the hardest step.

steps.note: User opens send money.
Quote generated.
User confirms quote — intent signal.
User starts adding a new recipient.
User picks: Self, Individual, or Business.
User enters recipient name and basic info.
User enters IBAN/SWIFT/account number. HARDEST STEP — many users don't have these details handy.
Recipient saved and selected for the transfer.
User picks payment method.
Final review and confirmation.
Transaction accepted.

critical_dropoffs: Quote Confirmed → Recipient Creation Started: many users go to recipient list instead
Recipient Type Selected → Recipient Info Added: form friction
Recipient Info Added → Recipient Bank Details Added: users don't have bank details

registration.name: Registration
registration.description: New user registration from account creation to MT profile. Events fire on all platforms but the journey differs (mobile has more granular steps).

steps.note: User begins signup. Web: 22k/week, iOS: 8k, Android: 8k.
Email + password submitted.
Verification email sent.
User clicks email verification link. BE event.
Name, DOB, address entered. MAJOR DROP-OFF: iOS 4,371 → 1,333.
Phone verified. Another drop — mobile: 1,284 → 14 to Country Confirmed.
Country selected for MT profile.
Profile submitted. Drops to 0 after this — MT Profile Created is BE-side.
BE event: Cognito confirms account creation.
BE event: DFX creates money transfer profile. Required to send money.

registration.note: 0% end-to-end in 7-day funnel because Profile Created → Account Creation Complete → MT Profile Created involves BE processing with variable timing. Check each step individually.
critical_dropoffs: Email Verified → Personal Details: major form friction
Phone Number Completed → Country Confirmed: massive drop (1,284 → 14 on iOS) — possible tracking gap or flow difference

login.name: Login
login.description: User authentication flow including 2FA. Login Accessed fires on app open (mobile) and page view (web).

steps.note: Login screen viewed. Mobile: fires on every app open. Web: login page loaded.
User submits credentials.
Server requires 2FA. Conditional — not all logins need 2FA.
2FA code validated.
Authentication complete. MOBILE ONLY event name — web may use a different name.

login.note: Low end-to-end because: (1) Login Accessed fires on every app open, most users are already logged in, (2) 2FA is conditional — most users don't need it, (3) Login Successful may not fire for all login methods on web.
issues: NAMING BUG: Web fires 'Login Failed' (capital F), mobile fires 'Login failed' (lowercase f) — TWO SEPARATE EVENTS in Amplitude
Login Successful fires mainly on mobile. Verify web equivalent.
critical_dropoffs: Login Accessed → Login Started: most app opens don't need login (already authenticated)
2FA Required → 2FA Successful: 2FA friction — iOS: 3,806 → 439 (11.5% success)

ekyc_verification.name: eKYC / Biometric Verification
ekyc_verification.description: Identity verification via Veriff or Onfido. Required for first-time senders or users exceeding transfer limits. Triggered mid-send-money flow.

steps.note: Verification SDK launched. iOS: 367, Web: 270, Android: 263 per week.
User consents to biometric data collection.
User completes document/selfie capture in Veriff/Onfido.
Client-side confirmation. Server result comes separately via 'eKYC Results Received'.
After verification, user returns to send money flow.
Transaction accepted.

ekyc_verification.note: Low volume but critical for new user conversion. Web has better completion rate, likely because desktop cameras/uploading is easier.
critical_dropoffs: Started → Consent: massive drop (367 → 35 on iOS) — users abandon when asked for biometric consent
Consent → Submitted: SDK capture process — some users fail to complete photo/selfie

open_banking_payment.name: Open Banking Payment
open_banking_payment.description: Payment via open banking (Volt/PayTo). User is redirected to their bank to authorize payment. Backend events track the lifecycle.

steps.note: User confirms transaction with open banking as payment method.
BE: payment request sent to open banking provider.
BE: provider processes the payment.
BE: payment confirmed by bank.
Transaction accepted.

open_banking_payment.note: OB starter → completer is very high: iOS 67%, Android 56%, Web 60%. The drop is Transaction Summary → OB Started — most users chose a different payment method. Of those who start OB, most complete.
critical_dropoffs: Summary → OB Started: most users don't choose open banking
OB Attempted → OB Completed: bank-side failures (~10% drop)

apple_pay.name: Apple Pay Payment
apple_pay.description: Apple Pay payment path on iOS. Extremely high success rate (94%) once attempted.

steps.note: User confirms with Apple Pay selected.
Apple Pay modal presented and user authenticates.
Apple Pay authorization succeeds.
Transaction accepted.

apple_pay.note: Apple Pay has 94% success rate (2,280 attempted → 2,138 created). The 18.2% from Summary → Created is because most iOS users don't use Apple Pay — but those who do almost always complete.
critical_dropoffs: Summary → Apple Pay Attempted: most users use other payment methods
Apple Pay Attempted → Success: near-perfect (99.4%)

repeat_transfer.name: Repeat Transfer
repeat_transfer.description: User sends money to the same recipient again. Skips recipient creation and often uses saved payment method.

steps.note: User taps 'Send Again' on a previous transfer.
Quote page opens with pre-filled details.
User confirms the new quote.
Reviews and confirms.
Transaction accepted.

repeat_transfer.note: Web repeat transfers convert much better (15%) because web users are logged in and have established payment methods. Mobile repeat transfer → Quote Accessed has a big gap (2,887 → 136 on iOS) suggesting many users start but don't complete the quote page.
critical_dropoffs: Repeat Started → Quote Accessed: massive drop on mobile (2,887 → 136 on iOS)
Quote Accessed → Quote Confirmed: rate/amount review step

error_recovery.name: Error Recovery (Payment Failed → Retry)
error_recovery.description: When a payment fails, the user can change payment method and retry. Measures how well the app recovers from errors.

steps.note: Payment processing fails.
User taps 'Try Different Payment Method'.
User picks a new payment method.
Confirms with new method.
Transaction succeeds on retry.

error_recovery.note: Web recovers 41% of failed payments — excellent. Mobile recovers only 10-12%. The gap is Change Payment Method → Payment Selected: web users are better at switching methods. Improving mobile error recovery UX could recapture 20-30% more failed transactions.
critical_dropoffs: Payment Failed → Change Method: many users abandon instead of retrying (iOS: 587 failed, only 102 try another method)
Mobile recovery rate (10%) vs Web (41%): huge gap — mobile error recovery UX needs improvement

backend_transaction_lifecycle.name: Backend Transaction Lifecycle
backend_transaction_lifecycle.description: Server-side processing after client creates transaction. Fraud screening, payment processing, disbursement.

steps.note: Transaction accepted (server-side). All platforms.
BE: fraud screening begins. Not all transactions are screened.
BE: screening passes.
BE: funds received from sender (card charged, bank transfer received).
BE: money sent to recipient.
BE: recipient receives money. End of lifecycle.

backend_transaction_lifecycle.validated_conversion_7d: Disbursement and Transaction Completed don't appear in 7-day funnel — likely due to processing delays (bank transfers can take 3-5 business days). Use 30-day window.
backend_transaction_lifecycle.note: Fraud Check Started fires for ~9% of transactions (1,038 out of 11,437 on iOS). Payment Completed fires for ~7% of Txn Created. The low percentage suggests most payments are processed differently or these BE events have tracking gaps.
critical_dropoffs: Transaction Created → Fraud Check Started: only ~9% of transactions get fraud-checked
Payment Completed → Disbursement: processing delay, not a real drop-off

plaid_bank_linking.name: Plaid Bank Linking
plaid_bank_linking.description: US/Canada bank account linking via Plaid. Required for direct debit payments.

steps.note: Plaid verification begins — user authenticates with their bank.
Bank account verified through Plaid.
Verified bank account saved as payment method.

critical_dropoffs: Bank Verification Started → Completed: Plaid credential failures, user drops off during bank auth

bank_transfer_payment.name: Bank Transfer / Wire Payment
bank_transfer_payment.description: User pays via bank transfer (wire/ACH). In countries where Xe has multiple receiving banks, user must pick which bank to transfer to.

steps.note: User sees Xe's receiving bank options. Shows in countries where Xe has more than 1 bank (e.g., EU has 2). Properties: numberOfAccounts, sendCurrency.
User selects which Xe bank to wire to. Properties: bankAccountChosen (bank name). Alternative: 'Bank Selected Other Country' fires if user picks the alternate country's bank.
User acknowledges the wire transfer instructions (account number, reference, etc.).
User confirms the transfer.
Transaction created — user must now send the wire to Xe's bank.

bank_transfer_payment.note: Bank transfers are staged payments — the transaction is created but Xe waits for the wire to arrive. 'Funds Received' (BE event) fires when Xe receives the money. User sees bank details and must initiate the transfer from their own banking app.
critical_dropoffs: Bank Account Selection: user may not understand which bank to choose
Instructions Accepted → actual wire: user must leave Xe and go to their bank — this is where many drop off (unmeasurable in Amplitude)

zero_day_conversion.name: Zero-Day Conversion (Registration → First Transaction)
zero_day_conversion.description: New user registers and completes their first transaction on the same day. The ultimate acquisition metric.

steps.note: BE: account created.
BE: money transfer profile ready.
User starts their first send money flow.
User confirms first quote.
First transaction created.

zero_day_conversion.note: Most zero-day conversions show platform=(none) suggesting the Account Creation and MT Profile events don't carry the platform property. 476 out of 17,741 account creations resulted in same-day transactions (2.7%). Web is highest at 5.7% (9 out of 157).
critical_dropoffs: Account Creation → MT Profile: 17,741 → 4,646 (26%) — many accounts created but never set up MT profile
MT Profile → Quote Accessed: 4,646 → 2,263 (49%) — half of profile creators don't try to send same day
Quote Accessed → Quote Confirmed: 2,263 → 1,463 (65%) — decent for intent
Quote Confirmed → Transaction Created: 1,463 → 476 (33%) — recipient/payment/KYC friction

card_payment.name: Card Payment (New Card Addition)
card_payment.description: User adds a new debit/credit card and pays with it. Includes card validation, billing address, and 3DS authentication.

steps.note: User selects card payment.
User begins adding a new card.
Card validated and saved.
User enters CVV for the new card.
3DS challenge presented.
Card authorized.
Transaction accepted.

card_payment.note: Very low end-to-end because most Payment Method Selected users don't add new cards — they use saved ones. The real metric is New PM Started → Transaction Created. New PM Started → Added: 676 → 358 (53% on iOS) — card validation failures kill half of new card additions.
critical_dropoffs: New PM Started → New PM Added: 53% success (card validation errors)
New PM Added → CVV Entered: big gap (358 → 26 on iOS) — users may switch to other payment methods after adding card

recipient_creation.name: Recipient Creation (Bank Transfer)
recipient_creation.description: Creating a new bank transfer recipient. The hardest part of sending money — requires IBAN, SWIFT, and bank details that users often don't have.

steps.note: User starts adding recipient.
Self, Individual, or Business.
Name and basic info submitted.
IBAN/SWIFT/account number entered. HARDEST STEP.
Recipient saved and selected.

recipient_creation.validated_conversion_7d: Included in send_money_new_user funnel. Type Selected → Info Added: ~80%. Info Added → Bank Details: ~75%. Bank Details → Selected: ~70%.
critical_dropoffs: Bank Details Added is the key friction point — users need IBAN/SWIFT which they may not have
Mobile bank lookup features (Top Banks, IFSC Lookup) help — check if users who use lookup have better completion

add_funds_web.name: Add Funds to Balance (Web Consumer)
add_funds_web.description: Web consumer adds money to their Xe currency balance. Reuses SendMoney components but fires separate Add Funds events. Differentiated by TRANSFER_TYPE.FUND_BALANCE.

steps.note: User opens Add Funds page. Properties: currencyAccount, availableBalance, accountType, brand.
Optional: user switches target currency. Properties: previousCurrency, newCurrency, availableBalance.
User confirms the funding quote. Same event as Send Money but with balance-specific traits (send_currency, payout_currency).
User confirms on summary. Properties: fundCurrency (array of from+to), balanceAddedAmount, totalBalance, availableBalance, paymentMethod.
Funds added. Same properties as summary confirmed.

add_funds_web.note: Web uses the shared SendMoney flow but sets transferType=FUND_BALANCE, which triggers Add Funds-specific events on the summary and completion pages. QUOTE_CONFIRMED fires in this flow (same event as send money).

add_funds_mobile.name: Add Funds to Balance (Mobile)
add_funds_mobile.description: Mobile Add Funds flow — enters through check-prices-xe.ts with balanceFlow=AddBalance. Different from web: does NOT fire QUOTE_CONFIRMED separately.

steps.note: check-prices-xe.ts:548. Properties: location ('Currency account home' or 'Home'), accountType, brand, availableBalance, currencyAccount.

NOTE LOWERCASE 'confirmed'. send-summary-xe.ts:744. Properties: sendCurrency, payoutCurrency, balanceAddedAmount, totalBalance, availableBalance, paymentmethod. Conditional: deliveryMethod=FundsOnBalance AND paymentMethod≠FundsOnBalance.
send-finish.ts:498. Same properties as summary. Fires when orderNumber exists.

add_funds_mobile.note: Mobile near-zero conversion in funnel analysis likely because the Add Funds events use a shared quote flow — users who access Add Funds may complete via Transaction Created instead of Add Funds Completed if the flow routing doesn't properly distinguish balance adds from sends. Also note: mobile event is 'Add Funds Summary confirmed' (lowercase c) vs web 'Add Funds Summary Confirmed' — may be TWO SEPARATE EVENTS in Amplitude.
issues: POSSIBLE NAMING MISMATCH: Mobile fires 'Add Funds Summary confirmed' (lowercase c), web fires 'Add Funds Summary Confirmed' (capital C) — verify in Amplitude if these are separate events

convert_balance_web.name: Convert Balance (Web Consumer)
convert_balance_web.description: Web consumer converts between currency balances. Reuses SendMoney components with TRANSFER_TYPE.CONVERT_BALANCE.

steps.note: SendMoneyExchangeBalance.vue:536. Properties: location, accountType, brand, availableBalance.
Optional: user switches currency. Properties: previousCurrency, newCurrency.
User confirms conversion rate.
Summary page. Conditional: TRANSFER_TYPE.CONVERT_BALANCE.
Conversion done. Note: web constant is CONVERT_BALANCE_COMPLETED but actual event string needs verification.

convert_balance_web.note: Very low volume (28/week). NAMING INCONSISTENCY: Web constant is CONVERT_BALANCE_COMPLETED but the event string value may differ from mobile's 'Convert Funds Completed'.

convert_balance_mobile.name: Convert Balance (Mobile)
convert_balance_mobile.description: Mobile currency conversion. Shorter flow than web — NO summary confirmation event.

steps.note: check-prices-xe.ts:548. Properties: location, accountType, brand, availableBalance, currencyAccount.

send-finish.ts:498. Properties: sendCurrency, payoutCurrency, balanceAddedAmount, totalBalance, availableBalance, paymentmethod. Conditional: deliveryMethod=FundsOnBalance AND paymentMethod=FundsOnBalance (both sides are balance).

convert_balance_mobile.note: Mobile Convert Balance has NO summary confirmation event — goes directly from quote to completion. This is different from web which fires Convert Funds Summary Confirmed.

fund_balance_corporate.name: Fund Balance (Corporate ONLY)
fund_balance_corporate.description: Corporate-only flow for funding balance via bank transfer. Completely separate from consumer Add Funds. Uses FUND_BALANCE events, NOT ADD_FUNDS events.

steps.note: FundBalance.vue:343. Properties: fundCurrency, location, tbuId, region.
Optional: user selects bank provider. Properties: paymentType='bank_transfer'.
FundBalance.vue:752. Properties: fundCurrency (array), amount, tbuId, region.

fund_balance_corporate.note: FUND_BALANCE_ACCESSED ≠ ADD_FUNDS_ACCESSED. These are DIFFERENT events from DIFFERENT flows. Fund Balance = corporate bank transfer to Xe. Add Funds = consumer adding money to balance via card/bank.

convert_balance_corporate.name: Convert Balance (Corporate ONLY)
convert_balance_corporate.description: Corporate-only currency conversion. Separate from consumer Convert Balance.

steps.note: corporate/pages/convert: Properties: location, tbuId, tbuRegion.
Properties: convertCurrencies (from+to), convertAmount, tbuId, tbuRegion.

convert_balance_corporate.note: Corporate uses CONVERT_BALANCE events, consumer uses CONVERT_FUNDS events. Different naming, different properties (corporate has tbuId/tbuRegion).

currency_account_activation.name: Currency Account Activation
currency_account_activation.description: User activates a new currency account (e.g., opens a USD balance). High conversion — users who start this are committed.

steps.note: User begins activating a new currency.
Currency account active.

currency_account_activation.note: High conversion across all platforms. Android highest at 85.5%. Simple 2-step process with minimal friction.

revenue_pipeline.name: Revenue Pipeline (Backend Transaction Lifecycle)
revenue_pipeline.description: The full server-side lifecycle from transaction creation to money delivered to recipient. These are ALL backend events — no client code involved.

steps.note: Transaction accepted. Client-side success + server-side creation.
BE: fraud screening begins. Only ~11% of transactions are screened.
BE: fraud screening passes.
BE: Xe receives the sender's money (card charged, bank transfer received, open banking payment). For bank transfers, this can take days.
BE: payment processing complete. Triggers disbursement.
BE: money sent to recipient's bank/wallet/cash agent.
BE: recipient receives money. END OF LIFECYCLE. This is the TRUE revenue event — the transfer is done.

revenue_pipeline.validated_conversion_30d: 82,079 Created → 9,103 Fraud Checked → 2,047 Funds Received → 0 Disbursement/Completed in funnel
revenue_pipeline.note: Disbursement Sent and Transaction Completed don't appear in the funnel likely because: (1) they fire under different user IDs (system events), (2) processing delays exceed the conversion window, or (3) they're tracked on a different Amplitude identity. Transaction Completed has 57k+ events per week independently, so they DO fire — just not linkable in a user-level funnel.

transaction_completed_breakdown.note: bank_account (34%) is the biggest — mostly bank/wire transfers. debit (30%) and direct_debit (24%) are card-based. Transaction Completed has 149 properties including revenueUSD, fee, totalCost, rate — the richest backend event.

key_properties.revenueUSD: Revenue in USD — THE key financial metric
key_properties.sendAmountUSD: Send amount in USD for standardization
key_properties.paymentMethod: bank_account, debit, direct_debit, open_banking, credit, balance

critical_dropoffs: Transaction Created → Fraud Check: only 11% get fraud-checked — most pass without screening
Funds Received appears low (2,047) relative to Created (82,079) — bank transfers take days, card payments are instant
Disbursement/Completed not in funnel — likely identity linking issue, use these events independently
